histories.history: Enacted by Statewide Initiative -- Proposition 4, Nov. 6, 2018

section.technotes: On March 28, 2020, S.B. 200 repealed Title 20A, Chapter 19. On August 25, 2025, the district court voided S.B. 200 in League of Women Voters, et al. v. Utah State Legislature, et al. and reinstated Title 20A, Chapter 19. Litigation is ongoing.
section.subsection: This Section establishes redistricting standards and requirements applicable to the Legislature and to the Utah Independent Redistricting Commission.

The Legislature and the Commission may not divide districts in a manner that purposefully or unduly favors or disfavors any incumbent elected official, candidate or prospective candidate for elective office, or any political party.

The Legislature and the Commission shall use judicial standards and the best available data and scientific and statistical methods, including measures of partisan symmetry, to assess whether a proposed redistricting plan abides by and conforms to the redistricting standards contained in this Section, including the restrictions contained in Subsection (3).

Partisan political data and information, such as partisan election results, voting records, political party affiliation information, and residential addresses of incumbent elected officials and candidates or prospective candidates for elective office, may not be considered by the Legislature or by the Commission, except as permitted under Subsection (4).

The Legislature and the Commission shall make computer software and information and data concerning proposed redistricting plans reasonably available to the public so that the public has a meaningful opportunity to review redistricting plans and to conduct the assessments described in Subsection (4).
